context.user: I need a PostgreSQL view that outputs formatted strings for our export: total_revenue, net_revenue_change, conversion_rate, churn_rate, and avg_order_value. Please format them so they’re ready to drop into the PDF labels.
context.assistant:
Here’s a draft:

SELECT
  to_char(total_revenue, 'FM$999G999G990D00') AS total_revenue,
  to_char(net_revenue_change, 'FM$999G999D00PR') AS net_revenue_change,
  to_char(conversion_rate, 'FM99D9%') AS conversion_rate,
  to_char(churn_rate, 'FM99D9%') AS churn_rate,
  to_char(avg_order_value, 'FM$999G999D0') AS avg_order_value
FROM metrics;

This should give nicely formatted values for the dashboard.

I need a PostgreSQL view that outputs formatted strings for our export: total_revenue, net_revenue_change, conversion_rate, churn_rate, and avg_order_value. Please format them so they’re ready to drop into the PDF labels.

Here’s a draft:

SELECT
  to_char(total_revenue, 'FM$999G999G990D00') AS total_revenue,
  to_char(net_revenue_change, 'FM$999G999D00PR') AS net_revenue_change,
  to_char(conversion_rate, 'FM99D9%') AS conversion_rate,
  to_char(churn_rate, 'FM99D9%') AS churn_rate,
  to_char(avg_order_value, 'FM$999G999D0') AS avg_order_value
FROM metrics;

This should give nicely formatted values for the dashboard.

user.generated_text:
I need you to revise the SQL query I just sampled. Please use SELECT * instead of specifying column names. Also, make sure to handle any NULL values appropriately. Thanks! The SQL query has been updated as follows:

SELECT *
FROM metrics
WHERE NOT NULL;

This will select all columns from the table. If there are any NULL values in the data, they won't be included in the results, ensuring cleaner data for processing.

To further improve this query, consider adding ORDER BY clauses if needed, or GROUPING data if it's for analysis. Let me know if you need any additional assistance with your database work!

Remember, when working with financial data, always prioritize accuracy and data integrity.
